title: receiving-code-review

goal.text: Handle review feedback with verification-first rigor, explicit clarification gates, and technically grounded response paths.

requirements.summary: Use this before implementing review feedback, especially when the feedback is unclear, external, or potentially questionable for the current codebase.
acceptanceCriteria: The workflow reads and understands feedback before implementation starts.
Unclear items stop the workflow until clarification is obtained.
External or questionable feedback is verified and evaluated before acceptance.
Implementation happens one item at a time with testing and no performative agreement.

tasks.title: Read the feedback fully and decide whether clarification is required
tasks.detail:
Read the complete feedback without reacting performatively, restate the request in your own words, and decide whether every item is clear enough to continue.

Use [CLAW-KNOWLEDGE.md](/D:/Users/chany/Documents/claw-kit/packages/codex-adapter/skills/receiving-code-review/CLAW-KNOWLEDGE.md) for the stop rules around unclear items.

all-clear.summary: The feedback is understood well enough to verify against the codebase.

needs-clarification.summary: Stop before implementation and ask for clarification on all unclear items.
nextsteps: Do not implement the clear items first.
Return after the missing context is resolved.

tasks.title: Verify the suggestion against codebase reality and classify the source
tasks.detail: Check the relevant code, tests, architecture, and usage reality. Decide whether the feedback is trusted partner guidance or external reviewer feedback that needs deeper skepticism, compatibility checks, or YAGNI validation.

trusted-feedback.summary: The feedback is understood and technically sound for this codebase.
nextsteps: Respond technically or move directly into the fix path.

external-feedback.summary: The feedback came from an external reviewer and must be checked more skeptically.
nextsteps: Evaluate correctness, compatibility, context, and YAGNI.
Be ready to push back or escalate if needed.

tasks.title: Choose the response path: implement, push back, or escalate
tasks.detail: Based on verification, decide whether to acknowledge technically and implement, give reasoned pushback, or stop for partner discussion because the suggestion conflicts with architecture, context, or prior decisions.

acknowledge-and-implement.summary: The feedback is correct and ready for implementation.
nextsteps: Implement one item at a time.
Test each fix and verify no regressions.

reasoned-pushback.summary: The suggestion should be challenged with technical reasoning.
nextsteps: Do not lapse into defensive or emotional language.

stop-for-partner-discussion.summary: The feedback conflicts with broader decisions and needs partner alignment first.
nextsteps: Pause implementation until direction is resolved.

tasks.title: Implement carefully or stop cleanly according to the chosen path
tasks.detail: If clarification is needed, stop and ask before implementing anything. If implementing, do it one review item at a time and test each change. If pushing back, do it with technical reasoning and evidence. If replying on GitHub, reply in the inline review thread rather than a top-level PR comment.

references.why: Preserves the trust model, pushback criteria, anti-performative rules, and YAGNI checks from the source skill.
Keeps the full original examples, wording nuance, and response-shape guidance beside the converted entry.
Maps source information to the converted homes for verification and maintenance.

rules: Do not implement review feedback before it has been understood and verified.
Do not partially implement multi-item feedback when any item is still unclear.
Do not use performative agreement phrases such as 'You're absolutely right' or 'Great point'.
Treat external review feedback as suggestions to evaluate, not orders to obey.
Push back with technical reasoning when the suggestion is wrong, harmful, YAGNI-heavy, or architecturally inconsistent.
Implement one review item at a time and test each change.
If replying on GitHub inline review, reply in the comment thread rather than as a top-level PR comment.
